qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary command execution in Execute Command node
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes the Execute Command node passing user input to child_process.exec() without validation. The advisory describes XML Node prototype pollution (CWE-1321) leading to RCE. These are different root causes: the finding's vulnerable code is ExecuteCommand.node.ts with a direct command execution sink, while the advisory's vulnerable code is the XML Node enabling global prototype pollution. No shared vulnerable function, parser state, or missing guard.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes XML Node prototype pollution (CWE-1321) in n8n leading to RCE when combined with other nodes. The sole qualifying finding (command-injection-in-execute-command-node) describes the Execute Command node's direct command execution via child_process.exec() — a different root cause, different code path, and different node. It does not involve the XML Node, prototype pollution, or CWE-1321.
evidence: Advisory root cause: XML Node enables global prototype pollution (CWE-1321) for authenticated workflow creators
Finding root cause: Execute Command node passes user input to child_process.exec() without validation — no XML parsing or prototype pollution involved
No finding references the XML Node, prototype pollution patterns (__proto__, constructor.prototype, Object.assign pollution), or CWE-1321
